title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: {{c1::How to reconcile the fact}}(如何调和这个事实) that they are {{c2::doing so well}}(表现这么好) on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are {{c3::pretty hard evals}}(相当严格的评估).”
original: How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals? You look at the evals and you go, “Those are pretty hard evals.”
translation: 如何调和这样一个事实：他们在评估中表现得如此出色？你来看看这些评估，就会想，“这些评估看起来相当难啊。”
note:
phrases: How to reconcile the fact | doing so well | pretty hard evals
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source: 
chunk: chunk_0005_f794b9
source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: stance_positioning | strong_collocation
transfer: Useful for signaling your stance before giving evidence.
expression_transfer: Useful for signaling your stance before giving evidence.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: I have {{c1::two possible explanations}}(有两种可能的解释). The more {{c2::whimsical explanation}}(奇思妙想的解释) is that maybe RL training makes the models a little too {{c3::single-minded and narrowly focused}}(专注而目标明确), a little bit too unaware, even though it also {{c4::makes them aware}}(让他们意识到) in some other ways.
original: I have two possible explanations. The more whimsical explanation is that maybe RL training makes the models a little too single-minded and narrowly focused, a little bit too unaware, even though it also makes them aware in some other ways.
translation: 我有两种可能的解释。更有趣的一种解释是，也许强化学习训练让模型变得过于专注，过于狭隘，在某种程度上让它们变得不那么敏感，尽管它也让它们在其他方面变得更加敏感。
note:
phrases: two possible explanations | whimsical explanation | single-minded and narrowly focused | makes them aware
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source:

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever We get used to things {{c1::pretty fast}}(相当快), {{c2::it turns out}}(结果发现). But also it’s {{c3::kind of abstract}}(有点抽象).
original: Ilya Sutskever We get used to things pretty fast, it turns out. But also it’s kind of abstract.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗尔 我们发现自己很快就能适应事情。但同时，这也有点抽象。
note:
phrases: pretty fast | it turns out | kind of abstract

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, {{c1::repeat itself twice}}(重复两遍) in some situation?
original: how can the model, on the one hand, do these amazing things, and then on the other hand, repeat itself twice in some situation?
translation: 模型怎么可能在一方面做出这些令人惊叹的事情，而在另一方面却在某些情况下重复自己两次呢？
note:
phrases: repeat itself twice

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Dwarkesh Patel {{c1::Should we actually begin}}(我们是否应该开始) here? I think this is an interesting discussion.
original: Dwarkesh Patel Should we actually begin here? I think this is an interesting discussion.
translation: Dwarkesh Patel 我们应该从这里开始吗？我觉得这是一个有趣的讨论。
note:
phrases: Should we actually begin

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever {{c1::Don’t you think}}(你不觉得) so? All this AI stuff and all this Bay Area… that it’s happening.
original: Ilya Sutskever Don’t you think so? All this AI stuff and all this Bay Area… that it’s happening.
translation: Ilya Sutskever 你难道不这么认为吗？所有这些 AI 相关的事情和硅谷的一切……它们正在发生。
note:
phrases: Don’t you think

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: I think the {{c1::impact of AI}}(人工智能对经济的影响) is going to be felt. AI is going to be {{c2::diffused through the economy}}(会逐渐渗透).
original: I think the impact of AI is going to be felt. AI is going to be diffused through the economy.
translation: 我认为 AI 的影响将会被感受到。AI 将会在整个经济中扩散。
note:
phrases: impact of AI | diffused through the economy

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: An example would be, {{c1::let’s say}}(假设) you {{c2::use vibe coding}}(使用振动式编码) to do something. You go to some place and then you {{c3::get a bug}}(遇到一个错误).
original: An example would be, let’s say you use vibe coding to do something. You go to some place and then you get a bug.
translation: 举个例子，比如你用 vibe coding 来做某件事。你去某个地方，然后遇到了一个 bug。
note:
phrases: let’s say | use vibe coding | get a bug
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: The idea that {{c1::we’d be investing}}(我们会投资) 1% of GDP in AI, {{c2::I feel like}}(我觉得) it would have felt like a bigger deal, whereas {{c3::right now}}(现在) it just feels... Ilya Sutskever
original: The idea that we’d be investing 1% of GDP in AI, I feel like it would have felt like a bigger deal, whereas right now it just feels... Ilya Sutskever
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗认为，如果我们将1%的GDP投资于人工智能，这本会是一件大事，但现在却显得微不足道。
note:
phrases: we’d be investing | I feel like | right now
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you tell the model, “{{c1::Can you please fix}}(能不能请你修一下) the bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. {{c2::Let me go fix}}(让我去修一下) that.”
original: Then you tell the model, “Can you please fix the bug?” And the model says, “Oh my God, you’re so right. I have a bug. Let me go fix that.”
translation: 然后你告诉模型：“能不能帮我修复这个bug？”模型会说：“哦我的天，您说得对。我确实有bug。让我去修复它。”
note:
phrases: Can you please fix | Let me go fix
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: This is one of the {{c1::very confusing things}}(非常令人困惑的事情) about the models right now. How to {{c2::reconcile the fact}}(接受这个事实) that they are {{c3::doing so well}}(表现这么好) on evals?
original: This is one of the very confusing things about the models right now. How to reconcile the fact that they are doing so well on evals?
translation: 目前模型的一个非常令人困惑的地方在于，如何解释它们在评估中表现如此出色的事实？
note:
phrases: very confusing things | reconcile the fact | doing so well
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Dwarkesh Patel I think your point, about how from the average person’s point of view {{c1::nothing is that different}}(并没有什么不同), will continue being true even into the singularity. Ilya Sutskever No, I don’t think so. Dwarkesh Patel Okay, interesting.
original: Dwarkesh Patel I think your point, about how from the average person’s point of view nothing is that different, will continue being true even into the singularity. Ilya Sutskever No, I don’t think so. Dwarkesh Patel Okay, interesting.
translation: 达瓦克什·帕特尔认为，从普通人角度来看，即使到了奇点时代，事物也不会有太大不同。伊利亚·苏茨克弗则认为并非如此。达瓦克什·帕特尔：有趣的观点。
note:
phrases: nothing is that different
title: Ilya-Sutskever

target_lang: zh

transfer: Useful for signaling your stance before giving evidence.
expression_transfer: Useful for signaling your stance before giving evidence.

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend dollar amount of investment. {{c1::I don’t think}}(我认为) anyone knows what to do with that.
original: Ilya Sutskever The thing which I was referring to not feeling different is, okay, such and such company announced some difficult-to-comprehend dollar amount of investment. I don’t think anyone knows what to do with that.
translation: 伊利亚·苏茨克弗尔提到的那种不觉得有什么不同的感觉是，比如某某公司宣布了一笔难以理解的巨额投资。我认为没人知道该如何利用这笔钱。
note:
phrases: I don’t think
title: Ilya-Sutskever

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: There’ll be {{c1::very strong economic forces}}(非常强大的经济力量) for this, and I think the impact is going to be {{c2::felt very strongly}}(感到非常强烈). Dwarkesh Patel When do you expect that impact?
original: There’ll be very strong economic forces for this, and I think the impact is going to be felt very strongly. Dwarkesh Patel When do you expect that impact?
translation: 这背后会有非常强大的经济力量推动，我认为它的影响会非常显著。达瓦克什·帕特尔 你预计什么时候会出现这种影响？
note:
phrases: very strong economic forces | felt very strongly

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: Then you tell it, “You have this new second bug,” and it tells you, “Oh my God, how could I have done it? You’re {{c1::so right again}}(又一次说得太对了),”
original: Then you tell it, “You have this new second bug,” and it tells you, “Oh my God, how could I have done it? You’re so right again,”
translation: 然后你告诉它：“你又出现了新的第二个问题”，它就会说：“天哪，我怎么会犯这种错？你又对了！”
note:
phrases: so right again

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce new RL environments and just {{c1::add it to the training mix}}(将其添加到训练数据中). The question is, well, what are those?
original: From what I hear, all the companies have teams that just produce new RL environments and just add it to the training mix. The question is, well, what are those?
translation: 据我了解，所有公司都有团队专门开发新的强化学习环境，并将其加入训练混合中。问题是，这些环境到底是什么？
note:
phrases: add it to the training mix
title: Ilya-Sutskever

target_lang: zh

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But when people do RL training, they do {{c1::need to think}}(需要思考). They say, “Okay, we want to have this kind of RL training for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
original: But when people do RL training, they do need to think. They say, “Okay, we want to have this kind of RL training for this thing and that kind of RL training for that thing.”
translation: 但当人们进行强化学习训练时，确实需要动脑筋。他们会说：“好的，我们想要这种类型的强化学习训练来解决这个问题，那种类型的强化学习训练来解决那个问题。”
note:
phrases: need to think
title: Ilya-Sutskever
source:

source_lang: en
target_lang: zh
phrase_types: phrasal_verb

title: Ilya-Sutskever
text: But there is another explanation. Back when people were {{c1::doing pre-training}}(进行预训练), the question of what data to {{c2::train on was answered}}(训练在...这个问题得到了回答), because that answer was everything.
original: But there is another explanation. Back when people were doing pre-training, the question of what data to train on was answered, because that answer was everything.
translation: 但还有一种解释。在人们进行预训练时，训练数据的选择问题已经有了答案，因为答案就是所有数据。
note:
phrases: doing pre-training | train on was answered
